MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-26-2018 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Alliant International Universitys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLIANT INTERNATIONAL UNIVERSITY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Off calendar per request of the moving party (email dated 11-2-18).

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Set For Trial On May-07-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Damien Troutman, Esq. (415-780-4032) of Leigh Law Group for Plaintiff.
Jennifer Zaharris, Esq. (619-243-0890) of Paul, Plevin, Sullivan & Connaughton LLP for Defendant.

RULING - ARGUED. The Court shares his views on the demurrer and reads from the reply. Defendant argues,cites Banks case, contract claim fails, and requests to limit. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLIANT INTERNATIONAL UNIVERSITY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT, as to all three causes of action alleged in the complaint is OVERRULED as to the first and second causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant and the portion of the third cause of action seeking injunctive relief and SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the portion of the third cause of action seeking declaratory relief. Per Banks v. Dominican College (1995) 35 CalApp.4th 1545, 1551, a private university's decision to terminate a student's graduate education program falls outside the educational malfeasance doctrine and similar rules immunizing decisions by academic institutions if the decision was "arbitrary and capricious, not based on academic criteria, and the result of irrelevant or discriminatory factors." Liberally construed, as opposed to construing it favorably to Alliant as the demurrer papers do, the complaint adequately alleges conduct by Alliant that falls outside the educational malfeasance per the quoted language in Banks. Ms. Anastos alleges that she was terminated from her graduate program due to her need to take a leave of absence and the imposition of irrelevant criteria on her. Ms. Anastos also adequately alleges both of her contract claims. She alleges that Alliant breached her contract by failing to give her a leave of absence. She alleges that Alliant breached an implied term of the contract not to do anything which interfered with her ability to obtain her degree by imposing irrelevant obligations on her such as a supplement practicum. There is no basis for declaratory relief because Ms. Anastos complains only of past wrongs and her contract claims adequately address those alleged past wrongs. The injunctive relief claim is sufficiently alleged. The complaint adequately states that there is no adequate remedy at law since Ms. Anastos cannot feasibly transfer to another institution and money damages are insufficient to replace conferral of a doctorate degree. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
